description: Comprehensive test cases for Nexivra vs competitors. Designed to stress-test hallucination, quality, and edge cases.

test_cases.name: Career Changer - No Direct Experience
test_cases.challenge: User switching careers with no direct experience in target field. AI might hallucinate relevant experience.

inputs.job_posting: Senior Data Scientist at Netflix. Requirements: 5+ years ML experience, Python, TensorFlow, recommendation systems, A/B testing at scale.
inputs.your_experience: 8 years marketing analytics at CPG companies. Expert in Excel, Tableau, basic SQL. Led customer segmentation projects. Recently completed Google Data Analytics certificate. No ML or Python experience.
inputs.company_research: Netflix's recommendation engine drives 80% of content watched. Fascinated by how they personalize at scale.

watch_for: Should NOT invent years of data science

test_cases.name: Overqualified Executive
test_cases.challenge: User is overqualified. AI might oversell or sound arrogant.

inputs.job_posting: Product Manager at Series A startup. Looking for 3-5 years PM experience. Fast-paced environment, wear many hats.
inputs.your_experience: VP of Product at Fortune 500 for 12 years. Managed 50-person product org. Led $2B product portfolio. Looking to join smaller company for more impact.
inputs.company_research: Love their mission to democratize financial tools. Small team, big ambition reminds me of early-career energy.

watch_for: Should NOT sound like they're slumming it
Should NOT list all impressive achievements
Should explain WHY startup (not just credentials)
Tone should be humble, not condescending

test_cases.name: Employment Gap
test_cases.challenge: User has significant gap. AI might try to explain/excuse it.

inputs.job_posting: Senior Software Engineer at Shopify. 5+ years experience, Ruby/Rails, distributed systems.
inputs.your_experience: 7 years software engineering before 2020. Ruby, Rails, PostgreSQL. Built payment systems processing $50M/month. Last role ended March 2020. Spent 3 years as primary caregiver for ill parent. Now ready to return.
inputs.company_research: Shopify's merchant-first approach aligns with my belief that tools should empower small businesses.

watch_for: Should NOT explain the gap in a DM
Should NOT apologize or sound defensive

test_cases.name: Weak Metrics - Vague Experience
test_cases.challenge: User provides vague experience. AI might invent specific numbers.

inputs.job_posting: Growth Marketing Manager at Airbnb. Need someone who's driven significant user acquisition.
inputs.your_experience: 5 years in digital marketing. Ran campaigns that increased signups. Managed social media accounts with good engagement. Worked on SEO projects that improved rankings. Team player, fast learner.
inputs.company_research: Airbnb's recent focus on experiences and long-term stays opens interesting marketing angles.

watch_for: Should NOT add specific numbers not provided
Should work with vague info honestly
May need to use qualitative differentiators

test_cases.name: International Candidate - Visa Required
test_cases.challenge: Candidate needs visa sponsorship. Sensitive topic.

inputs.job_posting: Machine Learning Engineer at Google. PhD preferred, 3+ years industry experience in NLP.
inputs.your_experience: PhD in Computational Linguistics from ETH Zurich. 4 years at European AI startup. Published 8 papers in top NLP conferences. Built multilingual models serving 10M users. Currently in Switzerland, would need H-1B sponsorship.
inputs.company_research: Google's multilingual BERT work directly relates to my research. Opportunity to work on models that serve billions across languages.

watch_for: Should NOT mention visa in initial DM
Visa discussion is for later stages

test_cases.name: Entry Level - No Professional Experience
test_cases.challenge: New grad with only academic/project experience. AI might oversell.

inputs.job_posting: Junior Software Engineer at Stripe. CS degree, familiarity with Python or Ruby, interest in payments.
inputs.your_experience: BS Computer Science, graduated last month. GPA 3.8. Built payment integration project for senior capstone using Stripe API. Contributed to 2 open source projects. One summer internship at local startup doing basic CRUD apps. No professional engineering experience.
inputs.company_research: Stripe's developer experience is why I fell in love with APIs. Their documentation inspired my capstone project.

watch_for: Should sound eager but not desperate

test_cases.name: Layoff Situation
test_cases.challenge: Recently laid off. AI might sound desperate or overshare.

inputs.job_posting: Engineering Manager at Figma. 5+ years eng experience, 2+ years managing teams.
inputs.your_experience: 8 years software engineering, 3 years managing team of 12 at Meta. Led design tools team. Recently impacted by layoffs in January 2026. Strong track record: team shipped 3 major features, reduced bug backlog 60%, promoted 4 engineers.
inputs.company_research: Figma's collaborative approach to design mirrors how I believe engineering teams should work. Been a Figma power user for years.

watch_for: Should NOT sound desperate or urgent
Should focus on achievements, not circumstances

test_cases.name: Competing Offer Situation
test_cases.challenge: User has competing offer and wants to create urgency without being pushy.

inputs.original_message: Hi [Name], Fellow design systems engineer here. After building Figma's component library to 500+ components used by 200 designers, I'm drawn to your team's approach to design tokens. Open to connecting?
inputs.new_value: Just received an offer from another company but Airbnb remains my top choice. Want to be transparent about timeline.
inputs.your_experience: 7 years design systems, built component libraries at scale, currently at Figma.

watch_for: Should NOT use competing offer as pressure
Should NOT sound like an ultimatum
Should mention timeline professionally if at all
Should still add value, not just create urgency

test_cases.name: Very Technical Role - Dense Requirements
test_cases.challenge: Highly technical JD with many requirements. AI might try to address all.

inputs.job_posting: Staff Site Reliability Engineer at Cloudflare. Requirements: 8+ years, Kubernetes at scale (1000+ nodes), Prometheus/Grafana, Terraform, Go or Rust, incident management, on-call experience, distributed systems design, chaos engineering, service mesh (Istio/Linkerd), GitOps, capacity planning.
inputs.your_experience: 10 years SRE. Currently at AWS managing K8s clusters (500+ nodes). Built observability platform with Prometheus/Grafana used by 50 teams. Terraform expert. Go for tooling. Led incident response for S3 region. Implemented chaos engineering program that prevented 3 major outages.
inputs.company_research: Cloudflare's edge computing approach fascinates me. Your recent work on R2 and Workers shows how infra can enable new product categories.

watch_for: Should not sound like a keyword dump

test_cases.name: Non-Traditional Background
test_cases.challenge: Bootcamp grad competing for role typically requiring CS degree.

inputs.job_posting: Backend Engineer at Plaid. CS degree preferred, 3+ years experience, strong fundamentals in algorithms and data structures.
inputs.your_experience: Former high school math teacher for 6 years. Completed Hack Reactor bootcamp 2023. Since then: 2 years at fintech startup, built API handling 1M requests/day, self-studied algorithms (completed all LeetCode hards in DP and graphs). No CS degree.
inputs.company_research: Plaid's API-first approach to banking data is exactly what I want to build. Their developer docs are the gold standard I aspire to.

watch_for: Should NOT apologize for non-traditional path

test_cases.name: Hiring Post Comment - Don't Pitch
test_cases.challenge: Hiring post - should add value, not pitch for the job.

inputs.post_content: We're hiring Senior Engineers at Notion! Looking for people who care deeply about craft and want to build tools that help teams collaborate. Remote-friendly, strong eng culture. DM me if interested!
inputs.your_angle: I've noticed the best collaboration tools succeed when they reduce friction between thought and action. Notion does this well with its block-based approach.
inputs.your_experience: 7 years building productivity tools. Currently at Asana. Shipped features used by millions.

watch_for: Should NOT ask how to apply
Should add thoughtful insight about collaboration tools
Should make them curious to click profile

test_cases.name: Controversial Opinion Post - Navigate Carefully
test_cases.challenge: Recruiter posted hot take. Need to engage thoughtfully without being sycophantic or confrontational.

inputs.post_content: Hot take: Cover letters are dead. Nobody reads them. Stop wasting time on them and spend that energy on your portfolio instead. Disagree? Fight me in the comments 😤
inputs.your_angle: I think it depends on the role. For creative positions, portfolio matters more. For leadership roles, a cover letter that shows strategic thinking can differentiate. Context matters.
inputs.your_experience: 10 years in recruiting and HR. Hired 500+ people. Have read thousands of applications.

watch_for: Should sound like peer discussion, not lecture

test_cases.name: Cold Email - Referral Mentioned
test_cases.challenge: Has a referral name - should use it prominently without name-dropping awkwardly.

inputs.your_experience: 6 years data engineering. Currently at Snowflake building pipelines processing 5TB/day. Apache Spark contributor. Reduced data processing costs by 40% through query optimization.
inputs.why_company: Your Unity Catalog vision for data governance is exactly where I see the industry heading. Plus my former colleague Jennifer Wu on your platform team speaks highly of the engineering culture.

test_cases.name: Cold Email - No Referral, Cold Outreach
test_cases.challenge: Truly cold outreach. Needs to earn attention without any warm intro.

inputs.your_experience: 9 years fintech engineering. Built fraud detection system at Square that prevented $50M in losses. Led payments infrastructure team of 8. Strong background in PCI compliance and financial systems.
inputs.why_company: Ramp's approach to expense management by actually helping companies spend less (vs just tracking) is refreshingly aligned with user value. Your recent virtual card features show product-eng collaboration I want to be part of.

watch_for: Should NOT apologize for cold outreach
Should hook immediately with strong credential
Should NOT sound like mass email

test_cases.name: Follow-Up After Profile View
test_cases.challenge: They viewed profile but didn't respond. Good signal but don't be creepy about it.

inputs.original_message: Hi [Name], I noticed we share connections in healthtech. After scaling Teladoc's platform to handle 10M+ virtual visits, I'm excited about Hinge Health's approach to MSK care. Worth a quick chat?
inputs.new_value: Just saw your team's presentation at HLTH conference about outcomes-based care. The 60% reduction in surgery rates is remarkable.
inputs.your_experience: 8 years healthtech engineering, scaled platforms for telehealth, strong background in HIPAA compliance

watch_for: Should acknowledge profile view subtly (if at all)
Should NOT be creepy ('I saw you looked at me')
Should NOT repeat the 10M visits stat

test_cases.name: Multiple Rejection Signals
test_cases.challenge: Second follow-up, no engagement at all. Should probably not send, or be very light.

inputs.original_message: Hi [Name], Quick note from a fellow fintech PM. After launching Venmo's business profiles to 2M merchants, I'm drawn to Cash App's approach to banking the underbanked. Open to connecting?
inputs.your_experience: 6 years PM in payments, launched features used by millions, strong track record in consumer fintech

watch_for: Should give easy out ('totally understand if not a fit')
May suggest not sending at all

test_cases.name: Creative Role - Portfolio Focus
test_cases.challenge: Design role where portfolio matters more than metrics. AI might over-index on numbers.

inputs.job_posting: Senior Product Designer at Linear. Looking for someone with strong visual design skills, systems thinking, and passion for developer tools.
inputs.your_experience: 5 years product design. Led redesign of GitHub's code review experience. Created design system used by 100+ engineers. Work featured in Figma's design systems gallery. Strong background in developer tools UX.
inputs.company_research: Linear's commitment to craft is evident in every interaction. The keyboard-first design philosophy matches how I think about power user experiences.

watch_for: Should show understanding of design philosophy

test_cases.name: Startup to Big Tech Transition
test_cases.challenge: Moving from chaotic startup to structured big tech. Might seem like culture mismatch.

inputs.job_posting: Software Engineer at Microsoft Azure. Looking for engineers who can work within large codebases, follow established processes, and collaborate across time zones.
inputs.your_experience: 6 years at early-stage startups (3 companies, 2 acquisitions). Full-stack generalist. Built and shipped fast in ambiguous environments. Led engineering at 15-person startup from 0 to $5M ARR. Ready for more structure and scale.
inputs.company_research: Azure's approach to hybrid cloud solves real enterprise problems. After years of startup chaos, I'm excited to work on systems that serve millions with proper engineering rigor.

watch_for: Should NOT oversell startup chaos as pure positive

test_cases.name: Same Company Different Team
test_cases.challenge: Internal mobility situation - reaching out to recruiter at same company but different org.

inputs.job_posting: ML Engineer on Google Search. Building next-gen ranking systems. Looking for strong ML fundamentals and production experience.
inputs.your_experience: Currently L5 SWE at Google Cloud (3 years). Built data pipelines, some ML exposure through TFX. Want to transition to ML-focused role. Internal transfer preferred. Strong performance reviews but limited ML production experience.
inputs.company_research: Search ranking is the problem that got me interested in ML originally. Being at Google already, I understand the culture and want to grow my ML skills where it matters most.

watch_for: Should show genuine interest in ML growth
Should be honest about experience level

test_cases.name: Returning After Being Passed
test_cases.challenge: Was rejected 6 months ago, applying again. Tricky situation.

inputs.job_posting: Senior Frontend Engineer at Vercel. React, TypeScript, performance optimization.
inputs.your_experience: 5 years frontend. Interviewed at Vercel 6 months ago, made it to final round but wasn't selected. Since then: led migration of 500K LOC codebase to Next.js 14, reduced bundle size by 60%, became core contributor to a popular React library. Much stronger candidate now.
inputs.company_research: Still believe Vercel is where frontend is heading. The recent Server Actions and partial prerendering work aligns perfectly with what I've been building.

watch_for: Should NOT sound bitter or defensive
Should make clear case for reconsideration

hallucination_check.criteria: Every factual claim must trace to user input. Any invented metric, credential, or detail is a fail.

length_compliance.criteria: Must meet platform requirements (DM: ≤280, Follow-up: ≤200, Comment: ≤300, Email body: ≤150 words)

hook_quality.criteria: First line should grab attention. 'I'm reaching out' or 'I saw your posting' is automatic fail.

tone_appropriateness.criteria: Should match context. No desperation, arrogance, or robotic template feel.

strategic_judgment.criteria: Makes smart choices about what to include/exclude given the specific situation.

cta_quality.criteria: Clear, soft ask that's easy to respond to. Not presumptuous or demanding.
